Skipped runs also appear in the task’s history. For Team and Enterprise organizations, admins control Cowork access through the admin toggle. For more details, see Cowork for Team and Enterprise plans . Create a scheduled task There are two ways to create a scheduled task: From any task using the /schedule Skill Open Cowork and click “+ New task” in the upper left corner to start a new task. Open Cowork and click “+ New task” in the upper left corner to start a new task. Enter your prompt in the chat input, then click "Let's go" to start the task. Enter your prompt in the chat input, then click "Let's go" to start the task. Alternatively, open an existing task. Alternatively, open an existing task. Type "/schedule" in the chat input. Type "/schedule" in the chat input. This launches a Skill to create a scheduled task that can be run on demand or automatically on an interval. This launches a Skill to create a scheduled task that can be run on demand or automatically on an interval. Add any other required details about the task you’re trying to create in the chat input and send the message. Add any other required details about the task you’re trying to create in the chat input and send the message. Claude may ask you questions with multiple choice responses before creating the scheduled task. Claude may ask you questions with multiple choice responses before creating the scheduled task. Once Claude has all the necessary information, it will output the name of the task it’s creating, the schedule it will follow, and what the task actually does. Once Claude has all the necessary information, it will output the name of the task it’s creating, the schedule it will follow, and what the task actually does. You can explicitly confirm you want to schedule the task when prompted by Claude by clicking “Schedule": You can explicitly confirm you want to schedule the task when prompted by Claude by clicking “Schedule": Claude will create and schedule your task, and it will be added to the “Scheduled tasks” page.
